subject: biology
note: SAMPLE pack demonstrating the PYQ schema + revision engine. Questions are factually correct and NEET-syllabus, but are NOT sourced from a specific paper (source = 'Sample (NEET pattern)'). Replace with verified real PYQs + SME-reviewed solutions and full 13-language translations before shipping; then remove the draft flag.

prompt.en: In a eukaryotic cell, the Krebs (citric acid) cycle takes place in the:

solution.en: The Krebs cycle occurs in the mitochondrial matrix. Glycolysis happens in the cytoplasm, and the light-independent reactions of photosynthesis occur in the chloroplast stroma — so those options are incorrect.

prompt.en: The natural pacemaker of the human heart is the:

solution.en: The sinoatrial (SA) node in the wall of the right atrium generates the impulses that set the heart's rhythm, so it is called the pacemaker. The AV node, bundle of His and Purkinje fibres conduct that impulse onward to the ventricles.

prompt.en: The structural and functional unit of the human kidney is the:

solution.en: Each kidney contains about a million nephrons, which filter blood and form urine. A neuron is a nerve cell, an alveolus is an air sac in the lung, and a villus is a projection of the intestinal lining.

prompt.en: Which nitrogenous base is present in RNA but absent in DNA?

solution.en: RNA contains uracil in place of the thymine found in DNA. Adenine and guanine are purines present in both DNA and RNA, so they are not the answer.

prompt.en: In vascular plants, water and dissolved minerals are conducted upward from the roots mainly by the:

solution.en: Xylem conducts water and minerals upward from the roots to the rest of the plant. Phloem translocates the sugars made in photosynthesis; the cambium is a meristem; the epidermis is a protective covering.

prompt.en: Insulin, the hormone that lowers blood glucose, is secreted by the:

choices.en: Alpha cells of the islets of Langerhans
Beta cells of the islets of Langerhans

solution.en: The beta cells of the islets of Langerhans in the pancreas secrete insulin, which lowers blood glucose. The alpha cells secrete glucagon, which raises blood glucose — the opposite effect.

prompt.en: Ribosomes in a cell are the site of:

solution.en: Ribosomes translate mRNA into polypeptides, so they are the site of protein synthesis. DNA replication occurs in the nucleus in eukaryotes; photosynthesis occurs in chloroplasts.

prompt.en: Double fertilisation — one male gamete fusing with the egg and the other with the polar nuclei — is a characteristic feature of:

solution.en: Double fertilisation — syngamy (male gamete + egg → zygote) plus triple fusion (male gamete + two polar nuclei → endosperm) — occurs only in angiosperms (flowering plants). Gymnosperms show single fertilisation.
